utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi , roger . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hey . good to see you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good to see you . are you ready to get started ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , i am .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: roger is a 62 year old male here for emergency room follow-up for some chest pain . so , roger , i heard you went to the er for some chest discomfort .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . we were doing a bunch of yard work and it was really hot over the weekend and i was short of breath and i felt a little chest pain for probably about an hour or so . so , i got a little nervous about that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and had you ever had that before ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , i never have , actually .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: whose mic is on ? i'm in .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and , um , how are you feeling since then ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , after , uh , we were done , i felt fine ever since , but i thought it was worth looking into .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and no other symptoms since then ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and any family history of any heart disease ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , no , actually . not , not on my , uh , uh , on my immediate family , but i have on my cousin's side of the family .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . all right . and , um , you know , i know that you had had the , uh , knee surgery-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: a couple months ago . you've been feeling well since then ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . no problem in , uh , rehab and recovery .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and no chest pain while you were , you know , doing exercises in pt for your knee ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no . that's why last week's episode was so surprising .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . and in terms of your high blood pressure , do you know when you had the chest pain if your blood pressure was very high ? did they say anything in the emergency room ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , they were a little concerned about it , but , uh , they kept me there for a few hours and it seemed to regulate after effect . so , it , it did n't seem to be a problem when i , when i went home .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and , and i see here that it was about 180 over 95 when you went into the emergency room . has it been running that high ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , usually no . that's why it was so surprising .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . all right . well , let's go ahead and we'll do a quick physical exam . so , looking at you , you know , i'm feeling your neck . i do feel a little enlarged thyroid here that's not tender . you have a carotid bruit on the right hand side and , uh , your lungs are clear . your heart is in a regular rate and rhythm , but i do hear a three out of six systolic ejection murmur . your abdomen is nice and soft . uh , there is the healed scar on your right knee from your prior knee surgery , and there's no lower extremity edema .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , let's look at some of your results , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , show me the blood pressure . yeah . and here , your blood pressure's still high , so we'll have to talk about that . um , hey , dragon , show me the ekg . so , here you- that's good , your , your ekg-

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . here is normal , so that's , that's very encouraging . um , i know that they had the echocardiogram , so let's look at that . hey , dragon , show me the echocardiogram . okay . so , looking at this , you know , you do have a little bit of a , a low pumping function of your heart , which , you , you know , can happen and we'll have to look into that , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , you know , my impression is is that you have this episode of chest pain , um , that could be related to severe hypertension or it could be related to some heart disease . so , what i'd like to go ahead and do is , number one , we'll put you on , um ... we'll change your blood pressure regimen . we'll put you on carvedilol , 25 milligrams twice a day . that helps with coronary disease as well as your pumping function of your heart . um , i wan na go ahead and order a cardiac catheterization on you and make sure that we do n't have any blockages in your heart arteries responsible for the chest pain .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: for the high blood pressure , we're gon na add the carvedilol and i want you to continue your lisinopril 10 milligrams a day and i wan na see , uh , how your blood pressure does on that regimen , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay . sounds good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . so , the nurse will be in soon and i'll ... we'll schedule that cath for you , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: you got it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , finalize the note .

detail_list.summary: Roger Nelson is a 62-year-old male presenting for emergency room follow-up for chest pain.
The patient experienced shortness of breath and chest pain for approximately one hour while doing yard work.
The doctor asks if the patient has a history of chest pain.
The patient denies a history of chest pain.
The doctor asks how the patient has been feeling since the chest pain episode.
The patient reports feeling fine since the episode.
The doctor asks if the patient has had any other symptoms.
The patient denies any other symptoms.
The doctor asks about family history of heart disease.
The patient denies a family history of heart disease in his immediate family.
The doctor asks how the patient has been feeling since his knee surgery.
The patient reports feeling well since his knee surgery and rehab.
The doctor asks if the patient experienced chest pain during physical therapy exercises.
The patient denies chest pain during physical therapy.
The doctor notes the patient's blood pressure was 180/95 mmHg at the emergency room.
The patient states his blood pressure is not usually that high.
Physical exam findings include a non-tender enlarged thyroid, right carotid bruit, clear lungs, 3/6 systolic ejection murmur, soft abdomen, healed knee scar, and no lower extremity edema.
The patient's EKG is normal.
The echocardiogram shows decreased ejection fraction.
Treatment plan
The doctor prescribes carvedilol 25 mg twice a day.
The doctor orders a cardiac catheterization.
The patient is instructed to continue lisinopril 10 mg daily.
